client_pipeline.cpp(3) Library Functions Manual client_pipeline.cpp(3)

NAME

client_pipeline.cpp - Unified client-side audio processing pipeline with WebRTC AEC3.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct WebRTCAec3Wrapper
C++ wrapper for WebRTC AEC3 (opaque to C code).

Macros


#define WEBRTC_APM_DEBUG_DUMP 0
#define WEBRTC_MODULE_AUDIO_PROCESSING 1
#define __STDC_NO_ATOMICS__ 1

Functions


client_audio_pipeline_config_t client_audio_pipeline_default_config (void)
Get default configuration. client_audio_pipeline_t * client_audio_pipeline_create (const client_audio_pipeline_config_t *config)
Create and initialize a client audio pipeline. void client_audio_pipeline_destroy (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline)
Destroy a client audio pipeline. void client_audio_pipeline_set_flags (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline, client_audio_pipeline_flags_t flags)
Set component enable flags. client_audio_pipeline_flags_t client_audio_pipeline_get_flags (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline)
Get current component enable flags. int client_audio_pipeline_capture (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline, const float *input, int num_samples, uint8_t *opus_out, int max_opus_len)
Process captured audio and encode to Opus. int client_audio_pipeline_playback (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline, const uint8_t *opus_in, int opus_len, float *output, int num_samples)
Decode Opus packet and process for playback. int client_audio_pipeline_get_playback_frame (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline, float *output, int num_samples)
Get audio frame from jitter buffer for playback callback. void client_audio_pipeline_process_duplex (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline, const float *render_samples, int render_count, const float *capture_samples, int capture_count, float *processed_output)
Process AEC3 inline in full-duplex callback. int client_audio_pipeline_jitter_margin (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline)
Get jitter buffer margin (buffered time in ms). void client_audio_pipeline_reset (client_audio_pipeline_t *pipeline)
Reset pipeline state.

Detailed Description

Unified client-side audio processing pipeline with WebRTC AEC3.

Implements production-grade echo cancellation using WebRTC AEC3 (Acoustic Echo Cancellation v3) with automatic network delay estimation, adaptive filtering, and residual echo suppression.

Uses WebRTC directly via C++ API - no wrapper layer.

Definition in file client_pipeline.cpp.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define __STDC_NO_ATOMICS__ 1

#define WEBRTC_MODULE_AUDIO_PROCESSING 1

Function Documentation

int client_audio_pipeline_capture (client_audio_pipeline_t * pipeline, const float * input, int num_samples, uint8_t * opus_out, int max_opus_len)

Process captured audio and encode to Opus. Encode already-processed audio to Opus.

In full-duplex mode, AEC3 and DSP processing are done in process_duplex(). This function just does Opus encoding.

Definition at line 444 of file client_pipeline.cpp.

445                                                                        {
446   if (!pipeline || !input || !opus_out || num_samples != pipeline->frame_size) {
447     return -1;
448   }
449 
450   // Input is already processed by process_duplex() in full-duplex mode.
451   // Just encode with Opus.
452   int opus_len = opus_encode_float(pipeline->encoder, input, num_samples, opus_out, max_opus_len);
453 
454   if (opus_len < 0) {
455     log_error("Opus encoding failed: %d", opus_len);
456     return -1;
457   }
458 
459   return opus_len;
460 }




References client_audio_pipeline_t::encoder,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::frame_size, and log_error.






client_audio_pipeline_t * client_audio_pipeline_create
  (const client_audio_pipeline_config_t * config)


Create and initialize a client audio pipeline. Create a new client
    audio pipeline.




This function:


    
  
  • Allocates the pipeline structure
    • 
  
  • Initializes Opus encoder/decoder
    • 
  
  • Sets up WebRTC AEC3 echo cancellation
    • 
  
  • Configures all audio processing parameters
    • 



Definition at line 156 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


156                                                                                                     {
157   client_audio_pipeline_t *p = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(client_audio_pipeline_t), client_audio_pipeline_t *);
158   if (!p) {
159     log_error("Failed to allocate client audio pipeline");
160     return NULL;
161   }
162 
163   // Use default config if none provided
164   if (config) {
165     p->config = *config;
166   } else {
167     p->config = client_audio_pipeline_default_config();
168   }
169 
170   p->flags = p->config.flags;
171   p->frame_size = p->config.sample_rate * p->config.frame_size_ns / 1000;
172 
173   // No mutex needed - full-duplex means single callback thread handles all AEC3
174 
175   // Initialize Opus encoder/decoder first (no exceptions)
176   int opus_error = 0;
177   p->encoder = opus_encoder_create(p->config.sample_rate, 1, OPUS_APPLICATION_VOIP, &opus_error);
178   if (!p->encoder || opus_error != OPUS_OK) {
179     log_error("Failed to create Opus encoder: %d", opus_error);
180     goto error;
181   }
182   opus_encoder_ctl(p->encoder, OPUS_SET_BITRATE(p->config.opus_bitrate));
183 
184   // TODO: investigate this claim from Claude: DTX stops sending frames during silence, perhaps causing audible
185   // clicks/beeps when audio resumes. For now leave it enabled.
186   opus_encoder_ctl(p->encoder, OPUS_SET_DTX(0));
187 
188   // Create Opus decoder
189   p->decoder = opus_decoder_create(p->config.sample_rate, 1, &opus_error);
190   if (!p->decoder || opus_error != OPUS_OK) {
191     log_error("Failed to create Opus decoder: %d", opus_error);
192     goto error;
193   }
194 
195   // Create WebRTC AEC3 Echo Cancellation
196   // AEC3 provides production-grade acoustic echo cancellation with:
197   // - Automatic network delay estimation (0-500ms)
198   // - Adaptive filtering to actual echo path
199   // - Residual echo suppression via spectral subtraction
200   // - Jitter buffer handling via side information
201   if (p->flags.echo_cancel) {
202     // Configure AEC3 for better low-frequency (bass) echo cancellation
203     webrtc::EchoCanceller3Config aec3_config;
204 
205     // Increase filter length for bass frequencies (default 13 blocks = ~17ms)
206     // Bass at 80Hz has 12.5ms period, so we need at least 50+ blocks (~67ms)
207     // to properly model the echo path for low frequencies
208     aec3_config.filter.main.length_blocks = 50;           // ~67ms (was 13)
209     aec3_config.filter.shadow.length_blocks = 50;         // ~67ms (was 13)
210     aec3_config.filter.main_initial.length_blocks = 25;   // ~33ms (was 12)
211     aec3_config.filter.shadow_initial.length_blocks = 25; // ~33ms (was 12)
212 
213     // More aggressive low-frequency suppression thresholds
214     // Lower values = more aggressive echo suppression
215     aec3_config.echo_audibility.audibility_threshold_lf = 5; // (was 10)
216 
217     // Create AEC3 using the factory
218     auto factory = webrtc::EchoCanceller3Factory(aec3_config);
219 
220     std::unique_ptr<webrtc::EchoControl> echo_control = factory.Create(static_cast<int>(p->config.sample_rate), // 48kHz
221                                                                        1, // num_render_channels (speaker output)
222                                                                        1  // num_capture_channels (microphone input)
223     );
224 
225     if (!echo_control) {
226       log_warn("Failed to create WebRTC AEC3 instance - echo cancellation unavailable");
227       p->echo_canceller = NULL;
228     } else {
229       // Successfully created AEC3 - wrap in our C++ wrapper for C compatibility
230       auto wrapper = new WebRTCAec3Wrapper();
231       wrapper->aec3 = std::move(echo_control);
232       wrapper->config = aec3_config;
233       p->echo_canceller = wrapper;
234 
235       log_info("✓ WebRTC AEC3 initialized (67ms filter for bass, adaptive delay)");
236 
237       // Create persistent AudioBuffer instances for AEC3
238       p->aec3_render_buffer = new webrtc::AudioBuffer(48000, 1, 48000, 1, 48000, 1);
239       p->aec3_capture_buffer = new webrtc::AudioBuffer(48000, 1, 48000, 1, 48000, 1);
240 
241       auto *render_buf = static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(p->aec3_render_buffer);
242       auto *capture_buf = static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(p->aec3_capture_buffer);
243 
244       // Zero-initialize channel data
245       float *const *render_ch = render_buf->channels();
246       float *const *capture_ch = capture_buf->channels();
247       if (render_ch && render_ch[0]) {
248         memset(render_ch[0], 0, 480 * sizeof(float)); // 10ms at 48kHz
249       }
250       if (capture_ch && capture_ch[0]) {
251         memset(capture_ch[0], 0, 480 * sizeof(float));
252       }
253 
254       // Prime filterbank state with dummy processing cycle
255       render_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
256       render_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
257       capture_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
258       capture_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
259 
260       log_info("  - AudioBuffer filterbank state initialized");
261 
262       // Warm up AEC3 with 10 silent frames to initialize internal state
263       for (int warmup = 0; warmup < 10; warmup++) {
264         memset(render_ch[0], 0, 480 * sizeof(float));
265         memset(capture_ch[0], 0, 480 * sizeof(float));
266 
267         render_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
268         wrapper->aec3->AnalyzeRender(render_buf);
269         render_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
270 
271         wrapper->aec3->AnalyzeCapture(capture_buf);
272         capture_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
273         wrapper->aec3->SetAudioBufferDelay(0);
274         wrapper->aec3->ProcessCapture(capture_buf, false);
275         capture_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
276       }
277       log_info("  - AEC3 warmed up with 10 silent frames");
278       log_info("  - Persistent AudioBuffer instances created");
279     }
280   }
281 
282   // Initialize debug WAV writers for AEC3 analysis (if echo_cancel enabled)
283   p->debug_wav_aec3_in = NULL;
284   p->debug_wav_aec3_out = NULL;
285   if (p->flags.echo_cancel) {
286     // Open WAV files to capture AEC3 input and output
287     char tmp[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
288     if (platform_get_temp_dir(tmp, sizeof(tmp))) {
289       char path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
290       safe_snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/aec3_input.wav", tmp);
291       p->debug_wav_aec3_in = wav_writer_open(path, 48000, 1);
292       safe_snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/aec3_output.wav", tmp);
293       p->debug_wav_aec3_out = wav_writer_open(path, 48000, 1);
294     }
295     if (p->debug_wav_aec3_in) {
296       log_info("Debug: Recording AEC3 input WAV to temp dir");
297     }
298     if (p->debug_wav_aec3_out) {
299       log_info("Debug: Recording AEC3 output WAV to temp dir");
300     }
301 
302     log_info("✓ AEC3 echo cancellation enabled (full-duplex mode, no ring buffer delay)");
303   }
304 
305   // Initialize audio processing components (compressor, noise gate, filters)
306   // These are applied in the capture path after AEC3 and before Opus encoding
307   {
308     float sample_rate = (float)p->config.sample_rate;
309 
310     // Initialize compressor with config values
311     compressor_init(&p->compressor, sample_rate);
312     compressor_set_params(&p->compressor, p->config.comp_threshold_db, p->config.comp_ratio, p->config.comp_attack_ns,
313                           p->config.comp_release_ns, p->config.comp_makeup_db);
314     log_info("✓ Capture compressor: threshold=%.1fdB, ratio=%.1f:1, makeup=+%.1fdB", p->config.comp_threshold_db,
315              p->config.comp_ratio, p->config.comp_makeup_db);
316 
317     // Initialize noise gate with config values
318     noise_gate_init(&p->noise_gate, sample_rate);
319     noise_gate_set_params(&p->noise_gate, p->config.gate_threshold, p->config.gate_attack_ns, p->config.gate_release_ns,
320                           p->config.gate_hysteresis);
321     log_info("✓ Capture noise gate: threshold=%.4f (%.1fdB)", p->config.gate_threshold,
322              20.0f * log10f(p->config.gate_threshold + 1e-10f));
323 
324     // Initialize PLAYBACK noise gate - cuts quiet received audio before speakers
325     // Very low threshold - only cut actual silence, not quiet voice audio
326     // The server sends audio with RMS=0.01-0.02, so threshold must be below that
327     noise_gate_init(&p->playback_noise_gate, sample_rate);
328     noise_gate_set_params(&p->playback_noise_gate,
329                           0.002f, // -54dB threshold - only cut near-silence
330                           1.0f,   // 1ms attack - fast open
331                           50.0f,  // 50ms release - smooth close
332                           0.4f);  // Hysteresis
333     log_info("✓ Playback noise gate: threshold=0.002 (-54dB)");
334 
335     // Initialize highpass filter (removes low-frequency rumble)
336     highpass_filter_init(&p->highpass, p->config.highpass_hz, sample_rate);
337     log_info("✓ Capture highpass filter: %.1f Hz", p->config.highpass_hz);
338 
339     // Initialize lowpass filter (removes high-frequency hiss)
340     lowpass_filter_init(&p->lowpass, p->config.lowpass_hz, sample_rate);
341     log_info("✓ Capture lowpass filter: %.1f Hz", p->config.lowpass_hz);
342   }
343 
344   p->initialized = true;
345 
346   // Initialize startup fade-in to prevent initial microphone click
347   // 200ms at 48kHz = 9600 samples - gradual ramp from silence to full volume
348   // Longer fade-in (200ms vs 50ms) gives much smoother transition without audible pop
349   p->capture_fadein_remaining = (p->config.sample_rate * 200) / 1000; // 200ms worth of samples
350   log_info("✓ Capture fade-in: %d samples (200ms)", p->capture_fadein_remaining);
351 
352   log_info("Audio pipeline created: %dHz, %dms frames, %dkbps Opus", p->config.sample_rate, p->config.frame_size_ns,
353            p->config.opus_bitrate / 1000);
354 
355   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT_AUDIO_PIPELINE(p, "audio_pipeline", NULL);
356 
357   return p;
358 
359 error:
360   if (p->encoder)
361     opus_encoder_destroy(p->encoder);
362   if (p->decoder)
363     opus_decoder_destroy(p->decoder);
364   if (p->echo_canceller) {
365     delete static_cast<WebRTCAec3Wrapper *>(p->echo_canceller);
366   }
367   SAFE_FREE(p);
368   return NULL;
369 }

client_audio_pipeline_config_t
  client_audio_pipeline_default_config (void )


Get default configuration.


Returns


Configuration with sensible defaults for voice chat


Definition at line 103 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


103                                                                           {
104   return (client_audio_pipeline_config_t){
105       .sample_rate = CLIENT_AUDIO_PIPELINE_SAMPLE_RATE,
106       .frame_size_ns = CLIENT_AUDIO_PIPELINE_FRAME_MS,
107       .opus_bitrate = 24000,
108 
109       .echo_filter_ns = 250,
110 
111       .noise_suppress_db = -25,
112       .agc_level = 16000, // Increased from 8000 for louder output
113       .agc_max_gain = 35, // Increased from 30 dB to handle very quiet mics (35 dB = ~56x gain)
114 
115       // Jitter margin: wait this long before starting playback
116       // Lower = less latency but more risk of underruns
117       // Must match AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD in ringbuffer.h.
118       .jitter_margin_ns = 20, // 20ms = 1 Opus packet (optimized for LAN)
119 
120       // Higher cutoff to cut low-frequency rumble and feedback
121       .highpass_hz = 150.0f, // Was 80Hz, increased to break rumble feedback loop
122       .lowpass_hz = 8000.0f,
123 
124       // Compressor: only compress loud peaks, moderate makeup for volume
125       // User reported clipping with +6dB makeup gain
126       .comp_threshold_db = -12.0f,            // Compress above -12dB (was -6dB)
127       .comp_ratio = 3.0f,                     // Gentler 3:1 ratio
128       .comp_attack_ns = 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT,    // Fast attack for peaks
129       .comp_release_ns = 150 * NS_PER_MS_INT, // Slower release
130       .comp_makeup_db = 6.0f,                 // Increased from 2dB for more output volume
131 
132       // Noise gate: VERY aggressive to cut quiet background audio completely
133       // User feedback: "don't amplify or play quiet background audio at all"
134       .gate_threshold = 0.08f,               // -22dB threshold (was 0.02/-34dB) - cuts quiet audio hard
135       .gate_attack_ns = 500 * NS_PER_US_INT, // Very fast attack
136       .gate_release_ns = 30 * NS_PER_MS_INT, // Fast release (was 50ms)
137       .gate_hysteresis = 0.3f,               // Tighter hysteresis = stays closed longer
138 
139       .flags = CLIENT_AUDIO_PIPELINE_FLAGS_ALL,
140   };
141 }

void
  client_audio_pipeline_destroy (client_audio_pipeline_t *
  pipeline)


Destroy a client audio pipeline.


Parameters


pipeline Pipeline to destroy (can be NULL)


Frees all resources including SpeexDSP states, Opus codec, and all
    work buffers.


Definition at line 371 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


371                                                                       {
372   if (!pipeline)
373     return;
374 
375   // Clean up WebRTC AEC3 AudioBuffer instances
376   if (pipeline->aec3_render_buffer) {
377     delete static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(pipeline->aec3_render_buffer);
378     pipeline->aec3_render_buffer = NULL;
379   }
380   if (pipeline->aec3_capture_buffer) {
381     delete static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(pipeline->aec3_capture_buffer);
382     pipeline->aec3_capture_buffer = NULL;
383   }
384 
385   // Clean up WebRTC AEC3
386   if (pipeline->echo_canceller) {
387     delete static_cast<WebRTCAec3Wrapper *>(pipeline->echo_canceller);
388     pipeline->echo_canceller = NULL;
389   }
390 
391   // Clean up Opus
392   if (pipeline->encoder) {
393     opus_encoder_destroy(pipeline->encoder);
394     pipeline->encoder = NULL;
395   }
396   if (pipeline->decoder) {
397     opus_decoder_destroy(pipeline->decoder);
398     pipeline->decoder = NULL;
399   }
400 
401   // Clean up debug WAV writers
402   if (pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_in) {
403     wav_writer_close((wav_writer_t *)pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_in);
404     pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_in = NULL;
405   }
406   if (pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_out) {
407     wav_writer_close((wav_writer_t *)pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_out);
408     pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_out = NULL;
409   }
410 
411   NAMED_UNREGISTER(pipeline);
412 
413   SAFE_FREE(pipeline);
414 }

  client_audio_pipeline_get_playback_frame (client_audio_pipeline_t *

int
  client_audio_pipeline_playback (client_audio_pipeline_t * pipeline,
  const uint8_t * opus_in, int opus_len, float * output, int
  num_samples)


Decode Opus packet and process for playback. Process network
    playback (decode and register with echo canceller as reference)


Definition at line 465 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


466                                                                    {
467   if (!pipeline || !opus_in || !output) {
468     return -1;
469   }
470 
471   // No mutex needed - Opus decoder is only used from this thread
472 
473   // Decode Opus
474   int decoded_samples = opus_decode_float(pipeline->decoder, opus_in, opus_len, output, num_samples, 0);
475 
476   if (decoded_samples < 0) {
477     log_error("Opus decoding failed: %d", decoded_samples);
478     return -1;
479   }
480 
481   // Apply playback noise gate - cut quiet background audio before it reaches speakers
482   if (decoded_samples > 0) {
483     noise_gate_process_buffer(&pipeline->playback_noise_gate, output, decoded_samples);
484   }
485 
486   // NOTE: Render signal is queued to AEC3 in output_callback() when audio plays,
487   // not here. The capture thread drains the queue and processes AEC3.
488 
489   return decoded_samples;
490 }






References client_audio_pipeline_t::decoder,
    log_error, noise_gate_process_buffer(), and
    client_audio_pipeline_t::playback_noise_gate.


Referenced by audio_decode_opus().






void
  client_audio_pipeline_process_duplex (client_audio_pipeline_t *
  pipeline, const float * render_samples, int render_count, const float *
  capture_samples, int capture_count, float * processed_output)


Process AEC3 inline in full-duplex callback. Process AEC3 inline
    in full-duplex callback (REAL-TIME SAFE).




This is the PROFESSIONAL approach to AEC3 timing:


    
  
  • Called from a single PortAudio full-duplex callback
    • 
  
  • render_samples = what is being played to speakers RIGHT NOW
    • 
  
  • capture_samples = what microphone captured RIGHT NOW
    • 
  
  • Perfect synchronization - no timing mismatch possible
    • 



This function does ALL AEC3 processing inline:



  
1.

  
AnalyzeRender on render samples (speaker output)

  
2.

  
AnalyzeCapture + ProcessCapture on capture samples

  
3.

  
Apply filters, noise gate, compressor




Returns processed capture samples in processed_output. Opus
    encoding is done separately by the encoding thread.


Definition at line 522 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


524                                                                    {
525   if (!pipeline || !processed_output)
526     return;
527 
528   // Copy capture samples to output buffer for processing
529   if (capture_samples && capture_count > 0) {
530     memcpy(processed_output, capture_samples, capture_count * sizeof(float));
531   } else {
532     memset(processed_output, 0, capture_count * sizeof(float));
533     return;
534   }
535 
536   // Check for AEC3 bypass
537   static int bypass_aec3 = -1;
538   if (bypass_aec3 == -1) {
539     const char *env = platform_getenv("BYPASS_AEC3");
540     bypass_aec3 = (env && (strcmp(env, "1") == 0 || strcmp(env, "true") == 0)) ? 1 : 0;
541     if (bypass_aec3) {
542       log_warn("AEC3 BYPASSED (full-duplex mode) via BYPASS_AEC3=1");
543     }
544   }
545 
546   // Debug WAV recording
547   if (pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_in) {
548     wav_writer_write((wav_writer_t *)pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_in, capture_samples, capture_count);
549   }
550 
551   // Apply startup fade-in using smoothstep curve
552   if (pipeline->capture_fadein_remaining > 0) {
553     const int total_fadein_samples = (pipeline->config.sample_rate * 200) / 1000;
554     for (int i = 0; i < capture_count && pipeline->capture_fadein_remaining > 0; i++) {
555       float progress = 1.0f - ((float)pipeline->capture_fadein_remaining / (float)total_fadein_samples);
556       float gain = smoothstep(progress);
557       processed_output[i] *= gain;
558       pipeline->capture_fadein_remaining--;
559     }
560   }
561 
562   // WebRTC AEC3 processing - INLINE, no ring buffer, no mutex
563   if (!bypass_aec3 && pipeline->flags.echo_cancel && pipeline->echo_canceller) {
564     auto wrapper = static_cast<WebRTCAec3Wrapper *>(pipeline->echo_canceller);
565     if (wrapper && wrapper->aec3) {
566       const int webrtc_frame_size = 480; // 10ms at 48kHz
567 
568       auto *render_buf = static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(pipeline->aec3_render_buffer);
569       auto *capture_buf = static_cast<webrtc::AudioBuffer *>(pipeline->aec3_capture_buffer);
570 
571       if (render_buf && capture_buf) {
572         float *const *render_channels = render_buf->channels();
573         float *const *capture_channels = capture_buf->channels();
574 
575         if (render_channels && render_channels[0] && capture_channels && capture_channels[0]) {
576           // Verify render_samples is valid before accessing
577           if (!render_samples && render_count > 0) {
578             log_warn_every(1000000, "AEC3: render_samples is NULL but render_count=%d", render_count);
579             return;
580           }
581 
582           // Process in 10ms chunks (AEC3 requirement)
583           int render_offset = 0;
584           int capture_offset = 0;
585 
586           // Process only complete 480-sample frames. The worker thread guarantees
587           // render_count == capture_count and both are multiples of 480, so no
588           // partial frames should occur. If they do (defensive), the tail samples
589           // pass through unprocessed rather than corrupting AEC3 with padded data.
590           while (render_offset + webrtc_frame_size <= render_count &&
591                  capture_offset + webrtc_frame_size <= capture_count) {
592 
593             // STEP 1: Feed render signal (what's playing to speakers)
594             copy_buffer_with_gain(&render_samples[render_offset], render_channels[0], webrtc_frame_size, 32768.0f);
595             render_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
596             wrapper->aec3->AnalyzeRender(render_buf);
597             render_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
598             g_render_frames_fed.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
599             render_offset += webrtc_frame_size;
600 
601             // STEP 2: Process capture (microphone input)
602             copy_buffer_with_gain(&processed_output[capture_offset], capture_channels[0], webrtc_frame_size, 32768.0f);
603             wrapper->aec3->AnalyzeCapture(capture_buf);
604             capture_buf->SplitIntoFrequencyBands();
605             wrapper->aec3->ProcessCapture(capture_buf, false);
606             capture_buf->MergeFrequencyBands();
607 
608             // Scale back to float range and soft clip
609             for (int j = 0; j < webrtc_frame_size; j++) {
610               float sample = capture_channels[0][j] / 32768.0f;
611               processed_output[capture_offset + j] = soft_clip(sample, 0.6f, 2.5f);
612             }
613 
614             // Log AEC3 metrics periodically
615             static int duplex_log_count = 0;
616             if (++duplex_log_count % 100 == 1) {
617               webrtc::EchoControl::Metrics metrics = wrapper->aec3->GetMetrics();
618               log_info("AEC3 DUPLEX: ERL=%.1f ERLE=%.1f delay=%dms", metrics.echo_return_loss,
619                        metrics.echo_return_loss_enhancement, metrics.delay_ms);
620               audio_analysis_set_aec3_metrics(metrics.echo_return_loss, metrics.echo_return_loss_enhancement,
621                                               metrics.delay_ms);
622             }
623             capture_offset += webrtc_frame_size;
624           }
625         }
626       }
627     }
628   }
629 
630   // Debug WAV recording (after AEC3)
631   if (pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_out) {
632     wav_writer_write((wav_writer_t *)pipeline->debug_wav_aec3_out, processed_output, capture_count);
633   }
634 
635   // Apply manual AGC (simple pre-gain to boost quiet microphones)
636   // TODO: Replace with proper WebRTC AGC module for adaptive gain control
637   static int agc_call_count = 0;
638   agc_call_count++;
639   if (agc_call_count <= 3 || agc_call_count % 100 == 0) {
640     log_info("AGC check #%d: flags.agc=%d, agc_max_gain=%.1f", agc_call_count, pipeline->flags.agc,
641              pipeline->config.agc_max_gain);
642   }
643 
644   if (pipeline->flags.agc) {
645     // Convert dB to linear gain: linear = 10^(dB/20)
646     const float agc_pregain = powf(10.0f, pipeline->config.agc_max_gain / 20.0f);
647     for (int i = 0; i < capture_count; i++) {
648       processed_output[i] *= agc_pregain;
649     }
650     if (agc_call_count <= 3 || agc_call_count % 100 == 0) {
651       log_info("AGC: Applied %.1f dB pre-gain (%.2fx multiplier)", pipeline->config.agc_max_gain, agc_pregain);
652     }
653   }
654 
655   // Apply capture processing chain: filters, noise gate, compressor
656   if (pipeline->flags.highpass) {
657     highpass_filter_process_buffer(&pipeline->highpass, processed_output, capture_count);
658   }
659   if (pipeline->flags.lowpass) {
660     lowpass_filter_process_buffer(&pipeline->lowpass, processed_output, capture_count);
661   }
662   if (pipeline->flags.noise_gate) {
663     noise_gate_process_buffer(&pipeline->noise_gate, processed_output, capture_count);
664   }
665   if (pipeline->flags.compressor) {
666     for (int i = 0; i < capture_count; i++) {
667       float gain = compressor_process_sample(&pipeline->compressor, processed_output[i]);
668       processed_output[i] *= gain;
669     }
670     // Apply soft clipping after compressor - threshold=0.7 gives 3dB headroom
671     soft_clip_buffer(processed_output, capture_count, 0.7f, 3.0f);
672   }
673 }






References client_audio_pipeline_t::aec3_capture_buffer,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::aec3_render_buffer,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::agc,
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::agc_max_gain,
    audio_analysis_set_aec3_metrics(),
    client_audio_pipeline_t::capture_fadein_remaining,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::compressor,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::compressor,
    compressor_process_sample(), client_audio_pipeline_t::config,
    copy_buffer_with_gain(),
    client_audio_pipeline_t::debug_wav_aec3_in,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::debug_wav_aec3_out,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::echo_cancel,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::echo_canceller,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::flags,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::highpass,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::highpass,
    highpass_filter_process_buffer(), log_info, log_warn,
    log_warn_every, client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::lowpass,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::lowpass,
    lowpass_filter_process_buffer(),
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::noise_gate,
    client_audio_pipeline_t::noise_gate,
    noise_gate_process_buffer(), platform_getenv(),
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::sample_rate, smoothstep(),
    soft_clip(), soft_clip_buffer(), and
    wav_writer_write().






void
  client_audio_pipeline_reset (client_audio_pipeline_t *
  pipeline)


Reset pipeline state. Reset pipeline state


Definition at line 687 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


687                                                                     {
688   if (!pipeline)
689     return;
690 
691   // Reset global counters
692   g_render_frames_fed.store(0, std::memory_order_relaxed);
693   g_max_render_rms.store(0.0f, std::memory_order_relaxed);
694 
695   log_info("Pipeline state reset");
696 }






References log_info.






void
  client_audio_pipeline_set_flags (client_audio_pipeline_t * pipeline,
  client_audio_pipeline_flags_t flags)


Set component enable flags.


Parameters


pipeline Pipeline instance


flags New flags to set


Thread-safe. Changes take effect on next capture/playback
  call.


Definition at line 420 of file
  client_pipeline.cpp.


420                                                                                                              {
421   if (!pipeline)
422     return;
423   // No mutex needed - flags are only read by capture thread
424   pipeline->flags = flags;
425 }
